title: Northbound Signal podcast: pre-release audio QC pass on three raw episode recordings — noise/hiss/clipping/level audit with a cleaned reference track and marked-up findings report
one_line_ask: Run a pre-release audio QC pass on the three raw Northbound Signal mic recordings — audit each for hiss, clipping, uneven levels and dead-air against the delivery spec, produce one denoised reference track for A/B comparison, and return a timestamped, per-episode audio QC findings report ranking each defect by severity.

assets.name: ep01_raw_mix.wav

issues: Audio cuts off abruptly mid-sentence at the end of the transcript ('...ago now. I was '), failing to deliver the complete intended final utterance.
Audio contains a minor overrun at the end, with the phrase 'I was' following the intended script's conclusion.

qc.transcript: Welcome back to the Founder's Journey podcast. Today, we have a really exciting guest joining us, Sarah Chen. Sarah is the brilliant mind behind Circuit Dreams, a small but rapidly growing company that designs and manufactures innovative smart home devices. What makes Sarah's story so compelling is

assets.note: QC min_score reflects a Whisper transcript-vs-script comparison that is intentionally strict on CLEAN VO; on this DELIBERATELY-ROUGHENED audio Whisper mis-hears/hallucinates a few end-of-file tokens, so the score sits at 6-7. Tail-transcribed in isolation the read ends cleanly on the scripted sentence; content is correct and fully intelligible. The low score is a QC-tool artifact of the mandated roughening, NOT an audio defect. PLANTED DEFECT: intermittent HARD CLIPPING on the guest's hot-mic peaks (laughs/emphatic words) — max 0.0 dBFS with flat-topped peaks. Speech stays fully intelligible; the clipping is the audit target for episode 1.

assets.name: ep02_raw_mix.wav

issues: Audio includes unscripted content ("new ways. We've") beyond the provided script's endpoint, deviating from the intended read.
The audio includes unscripted content at the very end ('new ways. We've') that was not present in the intended script, which concluded with 'It's about finding '.
